Rob Gomes mentions Maria van Castricum, mistress of Count Willem I, as his wife and mother of his children.

zh.Santpoort-Noord, Holland, Netherlands: 荷蘭努-拉藍德桑普爾吉努德
zh.Santpoort-Noord, Velsen, Holland, Nether: 荷蘭努-拉藍德桑普爾吉努德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode於1162年6月12日出生在荷蘭努-拉藍德維爾森時，他的父親Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode37歲，母親Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede27歲。他於大約1190年與Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1244年3月5日在荷蘭努-布拉班特可享年81歲，埋葬在荷蘭-拉藍德利斯包格。

zh-hans.Santpoort-Noord, Holland, Netherlands: 荷兰努-拉藍德桑普爾吉努德

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode于1162年6月12日出生在荷兰努-拉藍德維爾森时，他的父亲Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode37岁，母亲Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede27岁。他于大约1190年与Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1244年3月5日在荷兰努-布拉班特可享年81岁，埋葬在荷兰-拉藍德利斯包格。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

(iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode was born on 12 June 1162, in Velsen, North Holland, Netherlands, his father, Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode, was 37 and his mother, Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede, was 27. He married Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum about 1190. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 5 March 1244, in Cuijk, North Brabant, Netherlands, at the age of 81, and was buried in Rijnsburg, South Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode est né le 12 juin 1162, à Velsen, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum environ 1190. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 5 mars 1244, à Cuijk, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterré à Rijnsburg, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode am 12. Juni 1162 in Velsen, Nord-Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode 37 und seine Mutter Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede 27. Er hat um 1190 Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 5. März 1244 im Alter von 81 Jahren in Cuijk, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande und wurde in Rijnsburg, Südholland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode è nato il il 12 giugno 1162 nel Velsen, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum circa 1190. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 5 marzo 1244, nel Cuijk, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 81 anni, e fu sepolto a Rijnsburg, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederodeが1162年6月12日にオランダノオルド-hオランドベルセンで生まれた時，父Hugo van Teylingen van Brederodeは37歳，母Maria Danielsdr van der Merwedeは27歳であった。約1190年にMaria Gerardsdr van Castricumと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1244年3月5日にオランダノオルド-ブラバントクウイジクで81歳で死去し，オランダズイド-hオランドリジンスブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode(이)가 1162년 6월 12일 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 벨센에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1190년 Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1244년 3월 5일 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 c이j크에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 리j스버그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode nasceu em 12 de junho de 1162, em Velsen, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum aproximadamente 1190. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 5 de março de 1244, em Cuijk, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, com 81 anos, e foi sepultado em Rijnsburg, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode родился 12 июня 1162 г. в местности велсен, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum около 1190 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 5 марта 1244 г. в местности куиджк, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды в возрасте 81 год и был похоронен в местности риджнсбург, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Willem I van Teijlingen van Brederode nació el 12 de junio de 1162, en Velsen, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Hugo van Teylingen van Brederode, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Maria Danielsdr van der Merwede, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Maria Gerardsdr van Castricum aproximadamente 1190. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 5 de marzo de 1244, en Cuijk, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, a la edad de 81 años, y fue sepultado en Rijnsburg, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos.
